message:
chore: gitignore *.yml[.yaml].bak workflow backups + swarm coordination

Two ephemeral patterns kept surfacing in `git status` after agent
sessions that touched `.github/workflows/`:

- `*.yml.bak` / `*.yaml.bak` — the workflow-editing flow some agents
  use writes a `.bak` sibling alongside each edited workflow
  (`.github/workflows/ci.yml.bak`, `.github/workflows/fuzz-nightly.yml.bak`,
  …). The existing `*.mcp.json.*.bak` rule only covers MCP config
  backups, so these workflow backups were still showing as untracked.
  Broadening to the generic `*.yml.bak` / `*.yaml.bak` pair keeps any
  future workflow-script backup out of the repo while staying narrow
  enough not to collide with real project YAML.
- `.swarm/` and `.swarm_marching_orders.md` — multi-agent swarm
  orchestrator scratch directory (orchestrator.md, marching_orders.txt,
  ticks.log, review_orders.txt, phase.txt) plus the root-level briefing
  file the swarm launcher drops.

Matches the equivalent patterns in aadc, beads_rust, frankensqlite,
and remote_compilation_helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -60,6 +60,14 @@ ci-artifacts/
 proptest-regressions/
 test_meminfo
 
+# Workflow file backup copies (left behind by ad-hoc edits in .github/workflows/)
+*.yml.bak
+*.yaml.bak
+
+# Multi-agent swarm coordination scratch state
+.swarm/
+.swarm_marching_orders.md
+
 # Accidental shell output (stray numeric/short-name files)
 /[0-9]
 /[0-9][0-9]
